channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026: Empowering Community-Led Initiatives for Social Cohesion Across Asia
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026 is a transformative funding opportunity designed to strengthen social cohesion and promote inclusive societies across Asia. Launched through a strategic partnership between AVPN, Temasek Foundation, Silent Foundation, and the Nagulendran Philanthropy Alliance, this initiative is focused on bridging cultural divides, fostering collaboration, and supporting grassroots organisations that are actively […]

The post Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026: Empowering Community-Led Initiatives for Social Cohesion Across Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026 is a transformative funding opportunity designed to strengthen social cohesion and promote inclusive societies across Asia. Launched through a strategic partnership between AVPN, Temasek Foundation, Silent Foundation, and the Nagulendran Philanthropy Alliance, this initiative is focused on bridging cultural divides, fostering collaboration, and supporting grassroots organisations that are actively building unity in diverse communities.

In an increasingly interconnected yet fragmented world, social cohesion is no longer optional; it is essential. The Harmony in Diversity Fund recognizes that meaningful change begins at the community level. By empowering organisations that work directly with people from different cultural, ethnic, and social backgrounds, the Fund seeks to cultivate trust, mutual understanding, and long-term collaboration across Asia.

About the Harmony in Diversity Fund

The Fund is built on the belief that diversity should be celebrated and leveraged as a strength rather than a source of division. It aims to bring together stakeholders from across sectors; including philanthropic organisations, private institutions, public bodies, and grassroots communities; to collectively mobilize resources and scale impactful initiatives.

Through this collaborative approach, the Fund supports innovative and scalable projects that promote harmony and inclusivity. It also serves as a platform for knowledge-sharing and partnership-building, enabling organisations to learn from one another and amplify their impact.

Key Objectives

The Harmony in Diversity Fund is designed to:

  • Foster stronger relationships among diverse communities
  • Promote intercultural understanding and respect
  • Build capacity within communities to sustain harmony initiatives
  • Encourage innovation in addressing social cohesion challenges
  • Strengthen cross-sector partnerships for long-term impact

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for funding, organisations must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a legally registered non-profit organisation or social enterprise
  • Operate within Southeast Asia
  • Demonstrate active involvement in harmony or social cohesion initiatives
  • Provide at least two recent years of audited financial reports

However, the following entities are not eligible:

  • Religious organisations
  • Politically affiliated organisations
  • Organisations listed under US or EU sanctions or blacklisted by international agencies

Impact Areas

Applicants are encouraged to propose initiatives that fall within one or more of the following focus areas:

  • Promoting intercultural understanding
  • Strengthening collaboration among diverse groups
  • Building community capacity for harmony initiatives
  • Developing creative and innovative solutions to advance social cohesion

Projects can be at an early stage of development or ready to scale, as long as they demonstrate clear potential for impact and sustainability.

Funding and Support

While specific funding amounts are not explicitly stated, selected organisations will receive financial support along with opportunities for partnership, visibility, and ecosystem engagement. The Fund emphasizes not just financial aid, but also the strategic support needed to grow and sustain impactful initiatives over time.

Application Timeline

  • 16 March 2026 – Call for Applications Opens
  • 25 March 2026 – Virtual Information Session 1
  • 8 April 2026 – Virtual Information Session 2
  • May – July 2026 – Review Period
  • August 2026 – Announcement of Selected Grantees

Application Process

Applications are submitted through the Good Grants platform. Each submission will be evaluated by a panel of experts based on:

  • Relevance to the Fund’s mission
  • Innovation and creativity
  • Feasibility and scalability
  • Potential for long-term impact
  • Strength of partnerships and community engagement

Applicants may also apply as a coalition, with one lead partner representing the group. This collaborative approach is encouraged and aligns with the Fund’s emphasis on partnership and shared impact.

Why This Fund Matters

Social cohesion does not happen by chance—it is intentionally built through trust, dialogue, and sustained effort. Across Asia, many organisations are already doing incredible work to bridge divides, often with limited resources. The Harmony in Diversity Fund seeks to amplify these efforts by providing the support needed to expand their reach and deepen their impact.By investing in community-led initiatives, the Fund contributes to a future where diversity is not just acknowledged but embraced as a foundation for stronger, more resilient societies.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here. 

The post Harmony in Diversity Fund 2026: Empowering Community-Led Initiatives for Social Cohesion Across Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Host a Global Stage: Apply to Host the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7) in 2027
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The UNESCO Global Network of Learning Cities (GNLC) has officially opened its call for applications for cities interested in hosting the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7). Scheduled for June 2027, this highly anticipated global gathering will bring together leaders, policymakers, researchers, and practitioners to shape the future of urban learning in an […]

The post Host a Global Stage: Apply to Host the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7) in 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO Global Network of Learning Cities (GNLC) has officially opened its call for applications for cities interested in hosting the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7). Scheduled for June 2027, this highly anticipated global gathering will bring together leaders, policymakers, researchers, and practitioners to shape the future of urban learning in an era increasingly influenced by artificial intelligence.

Under the forward-looking working theme “Learning Cities in the Age of AI: Towards Inclusive Digital Urban Futures,” ICLC 7 aims to explore how cities can harness the transformative power of AI to promote inclusive growth, lifelong learning, and sustainable urban development.

About the International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7)

The International Conference on Learning Cities is one of the flagship events of the UNESCO Global Network of Learning Cities. It serves as a global platform for exchanging knowledge, sharing best practices, and strengthening collaboration among cities committed to lifelong learning.ICLC 7 will focus on how artificial intelligence is reshaping urban life ;from education systems to workforce development ;and how cities can strategically adapt to ensure that no one is left behind in the digital transition.Participants will engage in discussions that highlight innovative approaches to:

  • Building sustainable and inclusive learning ecosystems
  • Integrating digital technologies into education and governance
  • Advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) at the local level
  • Strengthening lifelong learning frameworks within urban environments

A Strategic Moment in the GNLC 2027/2028 Cycle

ICLC 7 is more than just a conference; it is a key milestone in the upcoming GNLC thematic cycle for 2027/2028. This cycle places strong emphasis on the intersection of AI, urban development, and lifelong learning.Through participation and hosting, cities will gain access to:

  • Practical tools for leveraging digital innovation
  • Strategies for strengthening lifelong learning policies
  • Insights into building resilient, inclusive, and smart urban communities

This makes the conference a pivotal opportunity for cities to align their local development agendas with global priorities.

Why Your City Should Host ICLC 7

Hosting ICLC 7 is a prestigious opportunity that offers both immediate and long-term benefits. Selected host cities will not only gain international recognition but also position themselves as leaders in the global learning movement.

Key advantages include:

  1. Global Visibility and Recognition
    Hosting elevates your city’s profile on the international stage as a champion of lifelong learning and innovation.
  2. Strengthened Partnerships
    Cities will have the opportunity to build and deepen relationships with global stakeholders, including governments, institutions, and development partners.
  3. Local Development Impact
    The event can catalyze local innovation, inspire policy reforms, and enhance educational and digital infrastructure.
  4. Investment and Collaboration Opportunities
    Hosting attracts international attention, opening doors to funding, partnerships, and collaborative projects.
  5. Community Engagement
    It provides a platform to involve local communities in a shared vision of learning, inclusion, and sustainable growth.

What the Conference Will Deliver

ICLC 7 will provide a dynamic and engaging platform to:

  • Showcase successful learning city models and practices
  • Review progress made across GNLC member cities
  • Promote integrated approaches to lifelong learning
  • Encourage knowledge-sharing across regions and sectors

The conference is expected to gather a diverse audience, ensuring a rich exchange of ideas and experiences that will shape the future of learning cities globally.

How to Apply

Cities that are members of the GNLC are invited to submit their applications to learningcities@unesco.org to host ICLC 7.

Application Requirements:

  • A completed application form (available in Word or PDF format)
  • Official endorsement from the city’s mayor

Submission Email:

learningcities@unesco.org

Application Deadline:

15 May 2026

Late or incomplete applications may not be considered, so early preparation is strongly advised.

As cities around the world navigate rapid technological change, ICLC 7 presents a unique opportunity to lead conversations that will define the future of urban living. Hosting this conference is not just about organizing an event; it is about shaping a global vision for inclusive, intelligent, and sustainable cities.Cities ready to take bold steps toward innovation and lifelong learning are strongly encouraged to apply and be part of this transformative journey.

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Host a Global Stage: Apply to Host the Seventh International Conference on Learning Cities (ICLC 7) in 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science in Germany 2026 (Max Planck Institute) – Salary, Requirements, and How to Apply
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Max Planck Institute for Political and Social Science in Göttingen, Germany, is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Fellow (m/f/d) in Political Science within its newly established Department of Political Institutions and Conflict. This is a full-time, three-year research position designed for highly qualified scholars who are interested in interdisciplinary, data-driven research on political institutions, […]

The post Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science in Germany 2026 (Max Planck Institute) – Salary, Requirements, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Max Planck Institute for Political and Social Science in Göttingen, Germany, is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Fellow (m/f/d) in Political Science within its newly established Department of Political Institutions and Conflict. This is a full-time, three-year research position designed for highly qualified scholars who are interested in interdisciplinary, data-driven research on political institutions, conflict, and democratic transformation.

This postdoctoral opportunity is hosted under the broader umbrella of the Max Planck Society, one of the world’s leading research organizations, known for its excellence in scientific innovation, independent research, and global academic collaboration.

Overview of the Postdoctoral Fellowship

The Department of Political Institutions and Conflict focuses on understanding how political institutions shape conflict dynamics and democratic processes. The department explores critical global issues such as political violence, democratic erosion, and electoral integrity, with a strong empirical emphasis on the Global South.The position is designed for early-career researchers who are ready to contribute to cutting-edge academic research while developing their own independent scholarly trajectory within a collaborative and interdisciplinary environment.

Research Focus Areas

The department organizes its work around three major themes:

  1. Institutions and political violence
  2. Democratic politics in transformation
  3. Political economy of electoral integrity

Researchers are expected to engage with these themes through both quantitative and qualitative approaches. The department places particular emphasis on:

  • Survey-based research
  • Observational causal inference
  • Fieldwork and interviews
  • Analysis of administrative and event data

Key Responsibilities

Selected candidates will be expected to:

  • Conduct high-quality independent and collaborative research aligned with the department’s themes
  • Co-author academic articles with department members
  • Participate in fieldwork and conduct interviews in relevant research locations
  • Support the recruitment of PhD students and junior researchers
  • Co-supervise and mentor junior researchers and doctoral candidates
  • Assist in administrative and academic coordination tasks, including:
    • Organizing workshops
    • Managing data
    • Preparing project deliverables
    • Disseminating research findings to broader audiences
  • Actively participate in the department’s research environment
  • Maintain regular in-person presence in Göttingen, as residence within commuting distance is required

Candidates must have:

  • A PhD degree (or equivalent) in Political Science completed before the start date
  • A strong publication record in high-quality international journals or demonstrated potential for such output
  • Proven interest in the department’s research themes
  • Strong quantitative research skills, including:
    • Panel regression analysis
    • Handling large administrative datasets
    • Survey data analysis
    • Statistical programming in tools such as R or Stata
  • Excellent written and spoken English communication skills
  • Strong analytical thinking and independent research ability
  • A commitment to academic integrity and transparency
  • Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in interdisciplinary teams

Desirable Qualifications

The following will strengthen an application:

  • Familiarity with causal inference methodologies
  • Regional expertise in Global South contexts such as West Africa or South Asia
  • Additional language skills relevant to research regions (e.g., Hindi or Bengali)
  • Experience collecting, coding, and analyzing event data
  • Experience conducting fieldwork and qualitative interviews

Benefits of the Position

  • Full-time postdoctoral appointment for three years
  • Competitive salary aligned with the German public service wage agreement (TVöD Bund, EG 13), depending on experience
  • Access to a globally recognized research environment with advanced academic resources
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration and international networking
  • No formal teaching obligations, allowing full focus on research
  • Possibility to engage with universities and teaching activities if desired

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a single PDF file including:

  • Curriculum Vitae with a list of publications
  • A two-page cover letter outlining research interests and alignment with the department
  • A two-page research proposal related to the department’s themes
  • Contact details of three referees
  • One writing sample (published article or equivalent), with clarification of contribution if co-authored

Only complete applications submitted in a single PDF will be considered. No additional materials should be included.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: May 3, 2026
  • Interview Dates (remote): June 11–12, 2026
  • Expected Start Date: September 1, 2026 (or earlier if agreed)

Additional Information

  • Applicants of all nationalities are eligible to apply
  • Women are especially encouraged to apply as part of efforts to improve gender representation in science
  • Applicants with disabilities are strongly encouraged to apply under equal opportunity policies
  • The position requires residence within commuting distance of Göttingen
  • Regular physical presence at the institute is mandatory

This postdoctoral fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity to work at a premier research institution, contribute to impactful global research on political institutions and conflict, and develop a strong academic career in political science. Candidates with strong quantitative expertise, research experience, and interest in global political dynamics are especially encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline: May 3, 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more fellowship opportunities , click here. 

The post Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship in Political Science in Germany 2026 (Max Planck Institute) – Salary, Requirements, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program 2026: Transform Communities Across Africa While Building Leadership and Career Skills
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program 2026 presents a powerful opportunity for young Africans who are passionate about making a meaningful impact in their communities while gaining life-changing professional and personal development experience. Designed to empower local talent, CorpsAfrica recruits, trains, and deploys young Africans to serve in rural and underserved communities within their own countries.This initiative […]

The post CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program 2026: Transform Communities Across Africa While Building Leadership and Career Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program 2026 presents a powerful opportunity for young Africans who are passionate about making a meaningful impact in their communities while gaining life-changing professional and personal development experience. Designed to empower local talent, CorpsAfrica recruits, trains, and deploys young Africans to serve in rural and underserved communities within their own countries.This initiative is not just about volunteering; it is about transformation, leadership, and sustainable development driven by Africans for Africa.

What is the CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program?

CorpsAfrica is a nonprofit organization committed to fostering a culture of volunteerism across Africa. Its core mission is to place highly motivated and capable young Africans in communities where they can collaborate directly with local residents to identify challenges and implement practical, community-led solutions.

Unlike traditional aid models, CorpsAfrica emphasizes grassroots development. Volunteers do not impose ideas; instead, they work alongside community members to co-create small-scale, high-impact projects that address real needs. These projects often focus on areas such as:

  • Agriculture and food security
  • Education and literacy
  • Public health and sanitation
  • Gender equality and empowerment
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Business and entrepreneurship
  • Water and sanitation systems

This approach ensures that every intervention is sustainable, culturally appropriate, and impactful.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program is a unique opportunity that goes beyond service. It equips you with essential life and career skills while deepening your understanding of your country and its development challenges.

Volunteers benefit from:

  1. Hands-On Community Development Experience
    You will work directly with rural communities, gaining real-world experience in project planning, execution, and evaluation.
  2. Professional Skill Development
    Volunteers receive intensive training in community integration, proposal writing, project design and management, and monitoring and evaluation.
  3. Personal Growth and Self-Discovery
    Living and working in a rural setting pushes you outside your comfort zone. This experience builds resilience, adaptability, leadership, and empathy.
  4. Diverse Networking Opportunities
    You will join a network of like-minded young Africans from various academic and professional backgrounds.
  5. Meaningful Impact
    You will create lasting change in communities that need it most.

Who Can Apply?

CorpsAfrica is looking for driven, compassionate, and resilient individuals who are ready to dedicate themselves fully to service.

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be at least 21 years old at the start of service
  • Be a citizen of the country where they are applying
  • Hold a university degree or have equivalent professional experience
  • Be willing and able to live and work in rural or remote communities for the entire service year
  • Understand that this is a volunteer opportunity rooted in service, not financial gain

Where is the Program Available?

CorpsAfrica currently operates in:

  • Côte d’Ivoire
  • Ethiopia
  • The Gambia
  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Malawi
  • Morocco
  • Rwanda
  • Senegal
  • South Africa
  • Uganda

If your country is not listed, applications may not yet be open.

Application Deadlines

  • Ghana – 30 April 2026
  • Kenya – 30 April 2026
  • Malawi – 30 April 2026
  • Senegal – 30 April 2026
  • Ethiopia – 15 May 2026
  • Morocco – 1 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early.

How to Apply

Visit the official website to apply.

The CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program is more than an opportunity; it is a calling for young Africans who are ready to lead change from within. By choosing to serve, you uplift communities while building yourself into a leader, innovator, and changemaker.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here .

For more global opportunities, click here

The post CorpsAfrica Volunteer Program 2026: Transform Communities Across Africa While Building Leadership and Career Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Africa Climate Collaborative Scholarships 2026/2027 at Makerere University (Masters and PhD)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are searching for a fully funded postgraduate opportunity in Africa that combines academic excellence, climate innovation, leadership development, and financial support, then the Africa Climate Collaborative Scholarships at Makerere University present one of the most competitive and transformative opportunities currently open. About the Africa Climate Collaborative Initiative The Africa Climate Collaborative is a […]

The post Fully Funded Africa Climate Collaborative Scholarships 2026/2027 at Makerere University (Masters and PhD) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are searching for a fully funded postgraduate opportunity in Africa that combines academic excellence, climate innovation, leadership development, and financial support, then the Africa Climate Collaborative Scholarships at Makerere University present one of the most competitive and transformative opportunities currently open.

About the Africa Climate Collaborative Initiative

The Africa Climate Collaborative is a partnership initiative supported by global development stakeholders to:

  • Promote African-led solutions to climate change
  • Support research, innovation, and entrepreneurship
  • Build future leaders in sustainability and environmental management

The program is implemented through the College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences (CAES) at Makerere University, which is known for its strong focus on agriculture, environmental science, and climate-smart systems.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of People)

This scholarship is intentionally inclusive and targets underrepresented and disadvantaged groups across Africa.

You are eligible if you belong to any of the following groups:

  • Refugees or internally displaced persons (IDPs)
  • Young people with disabilities
  • International students (from African countries)
  • Youth from refugee host communities
  • Individuals from minority tribes
  • Other economically disadvantaged African youth

Additionally:

  • 70% of scholarships are reserved for female applicants
  • 30% for male applicants

Eligible Countries and Regions

Geographic Eligibility

Applicants must have studied in or come from:

  • African countries (mandatory requirement)

This means:

  • Open to East Africa, West Africa, Central Africa, North Africa, and Southern Africa
  • Not open to applicants whose previous education was outside Africa

Available Fields of Study

The scholarship focuses on climate resilience and sustainability-related programs, including:

Master’s Programs

  • MSc in Soil Science and Agrology
  • MSc in Crop Science
  • MSc in Environment and Natural Resources Management
  • MSc in Agricultural Engineering
  • MSc in Renewable Energy
  • MSc in Livestock Development and Management
  • MSc in Technology Innovation and Industry Development
  • MA in Economic Policy and Planning
  • MSc in Global Biosecurity and Infectious Diseases Management

PhD Research Areas

  • Crop or livestock breeding
  • Water access for agriculture
  • Post-harvest handling and value addition
  • Ecosystem conservation (forests, wetlands, rangelands)
  • Renewable energy solutions
  • Climate finance and green investment
  • ICT for climate resilience
  • Waste management and circular economy

All research must contribute to climate resilience in agropastoral systems.

Eligibility Requirements (Detailed Breakdown)

For Master’s Applicants

To qualify, you must:

  1. Apply for admission at Makerere University
  2. Hold an undergraduate degree from an African institution
  3. Demonstrate financial need
  4. Not hold any other scholarship
  5. Not already have a Master’s degree
  6. Show leadership or community service experience
  7. Be 35 years or younger
    • Exception: Refugees and persons with disabilities (up to 40 years)
  8. Show interest in climate-related innovation
  9. Commit to completing the program within 2 years

For PhD Applicants

You must:

  1. Have both undergraduate and Master’s degrees from African institutions
  2. Not hold another PhD or scholarship
  3. Demonstrate financial need
  4. Show leadership experience
  5. Be 40 years or younger
    • Exception: Refugees and persons with disabilities (up to 45 years)
  6. Present a research focus aligned with climate resilience
  7. Commit to completing the program within 4 years

Special Requirements for Refugees

Applicants with refugee status must provide:

  • Refugee Identity Card OR
  • Family Attestation documents

This ensures priority inclusion of displaced populations.

Application Process (Step-by-Step Guide)

To apply:

Step 1: Visit the Official Portal

Apply through the official application system

Step 2: Create an Account

You will need:

  • Valid email address
  • Active phone number
  • Stable internet connection

Step 3: Apply for Admission First

Before scholarship consideration, you must:

  • Apply to an eligible program at Makerere University

Step 4: Complete Scholarship Application

  • Fill out all required fields
  • Upload supporting documents
  • Ensure accuracy (details cannot be edited later)

Step 5: Submit Before Deadline

  • Final submission deadline:
    5th June 2026 (11:59 PM EAT)

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

This scholarship stands out because it offers:

  • Access to a top African university
  • Opportunity to contribute to real climate solutions
  • Focus on leadership and social impact
  • Strong support for women and marginalized communities
  • A pathway into research, policy, and innovation careers

Key Takeaways

  • This is a highly competitive, impact-driven scholarship
  • Open exclusively to African students
  • Strong emphasis on equity and inclusion
  • Focuses on climate change and sustainability
  • Requires both academic merit and leadership potential

Deadline

Friday, 5th June 2026

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded Africa Climate Collaborative Scholarships 2026/2027 at Makerere University (Masters and PhD) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Study Social Sciences in Spain: MA Scholarships at Universidad Carlos III 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Master’s Degree in Social Sciences offered by Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, in collaboration with the Juan Linz Institute, is a highly rigorous and research-focused graduate program designed for students aiming to build strong academic and analytical foundations. This program stands out for its emphasis on quantitative methods, critical thinking, and interdisciplinary training across […]

The post Study Social Sciences in Spain: MA Scholarships at Universidad Carlos III 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Master’s Degree in Social Sciences offered by Universidad Carlos III de Madrid, in collaboration with the Juan Linz Institute, is a highly rigorous and research-focused graduate program designed for students aiming to build strong academic and analytical foundations.

This program stands out for its emphasis on quantitative methods, critical thinking, and interdisciplinary training across key social science fields such as political science, sociology, and economics. It is specifically structured to prepare students for competitive PhD programs and research careers at leading global institutions.

What makes this opportunity particularly valuable is the availability of multiple funding options, including fully funded scholarships and tuition grants. These are designed to support talented students and reduce financial barriers to accessing high-quality education in Europe.

The program is internationally oriented, taught in English, and attracts students from diverse academic and geographic backgrounds, creating a dynamic and intellectually stimulating learning environment.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Access to high-quality graduate education remains a challenge for many talented students worldwide due to financial constraints. This program addresses that gap by offering substantial funding opportunities alongside top-tier academic training.

By combining financial support with rigorous academic preparation, the program helps develop the next generation of researchers, policymakers, and thought leaders. It equips students with the tools needed to analyze and address complex global issues, from inequality and governance to economic development and social change.

Opportunities like this are critical in building a more inclusive academic landscape, where talented individuals from diverse backgrounds can contribute to global knowledge and research.

What You’ll Gain

Students in this program gain a strong academic and professional advantage through advanced training in research methods and data analysis. These skills are increasingly essential for careers in academia, public policy, international organizations, and research institutions.

Beyond technical skills, the program provides direct exposure to leading scholars and ongoing research projects, allowing students to engage with real-world social challenges. This significantly strengthens their academic profiles and prepares them for doctoral studies.

Financially, the scholarships and grants reduce or fully eliminate tuition costs, making the program accessible to a wider pool of talented applicants. This allows students to focus on their studies without the burden of financial stress.

Additionally, participants become part of a global academic network, opening doors to future collaborations, research opportunities, and career advancement.

Full Scholarships

The program offers three full scholarships funded by the Department of Social Sciences, the Juan Linz Institute, and Banco Santander. These scholarships are awarded to outstanding candidates based on academic merit and potential.

A key highlight is the IJL–Francisco Cantú Full Scholarship. This award is given to the top-ranked candidate among all full scholarship recipients and recognizes exceptional academic excellence. It honors Francisco Cantú, a highly respected scholar known for his impactful research on electoral fraud in Latin America and his contributions to political science.

The remaining full scholarships provide comprehensive financial support, ensuring that recipients can fully dedicate themselves to their academic journey without financial constraints.

Tuition Grants

In addition to full scholarships, the program offers three tuition grants funded by the Department of Social Sciences and the Juan Linz Institute. These grants cover tuition fees equivalent to those paid by European Union students.

While these grants do not cover full living expenses, they significantly reduce the overall cost of the program, especially for international students. This makes studying in Spain more affordable and accessible.

Applicants should note that tuition grants require a separate application process, and candidates are encouraged to apply for both funding options to maximize their chances of receiving support.

Eligibility

The program is open to applicants with a strong academic background in social sciences or related fields. Candidates are expected to demonstrate academic excellence, analytical thinking, and a clear interest in research.

While specific degree requirements may vary, applicants typically hold a bachelor’s degree and show potential for success in a rigorous academic environment. The program is open to international students, making it a competitive and globally accessible opportunity.

Strong motivation to pursue research, particularly for those considering a PhD, is highly valued in the selection process.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must first submit an application to the MA in Social Sciences program through the official platform.

For funding, applicants must apply separately for full scholarships and tuition grants. Each funding scheme has its own application process and requirements, which are clearly outlined on the official website.

Applicants should carefully prepare all required documents and follow the instructions provided to ensure their applications are complete.

  • The deadline for full scholarships is April 30, 2026.
  • The deadline for tuition grants is May 31, 2026.

Candidates are strongly encouraged to apply for both funding options to increase their chances of receiving financial support.

To learn more and apply, visit the official scholarship page here.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Study Social Sciences in Spain: MA Scholarships at Universidad Carlos III 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Life of Pachamama Youth Climate Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Life of Pachamama COP31 Fellowship 2026, titled “Democratizing the Future: Youth for Climate Action”, is a structured, fully-funded program designed for young leaders passionate about climate action and environmental justice. The program aims to equip participants with the knowledge, skills, and experience to participate in international climate negotiations and promote climate solutions in their […]

The post Apply Now: Life of Pachamama Youth Climate Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Life of Pachamama COP31 Fellowship 2026, titled “Democratizing the Future: Youth for Climate Action”, is a structured, fully-funded program designed for young leaders passionate about climate action and environmental justice. The program aims to equip participants with the knowledge, skills, and experience to participate in international climate negotiations and promote climate solutions in their local communities.

Life of Pachamama is a non-profit youth organization dedicated to mobilizing the current generation of climate leaders. The organization works to strengthen youth participation in environmental governance and to ensure that knowledge about climate justice and sustainability is accessible to communities worldwide, particularly in the Global South.

This third edition of the fellowship combines training, mentorship, global exposure, and practical application. Participants selected for the program will engage in a multi-stage process including online training, mentorship, participation at the 31st Conference of the Parties (COP31) in Antalya, Turkey, and the implementation of local climate initiatives after the conference.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change is a global challenge that disproportionately affects vulnerable communities, particularly in the Global South. Effective climate action requires not only knowledge but also the ability to implement local solutions informed by global negotiations. The Life of Pachamama COP31 Fellowship bridges this gap by equipping young leaders with the skills and exposure needed to influence climate policy and promote sustainable development at the community level.

The program fosters intergenerational dialogue, builds long-term leadership capacity, and strengthens local engagement with the UN’s climate framework. By translating international policy into actionable local initiatives, fellows play a critical role in promoting equity, environmental justice, and the democratization of climate knowledge.

What You’ll Gain

The COP31 fellowship offers a comprehensive set of benefits that extend beyond standard internships or educational programs. Participants will gain:

  • Professional experience and international exposure: Selected youth will engage with governmental and non-governmental stakeholders at COP31, observing multilateral climate negotiations and contributing to policy discussions.
  • Practical skills in climate action: Fellows will participate in intensive online training sessions led by climate experts, focusing on policy, negotiation strategies, advocacy, and project management.
  • Capacity building in knowledge democratization: Participants will learn to translate complex climate data into actionable proposals for local communities, strengthening community awareness and resilience.
  • Networking opportunities: Direct interaction with international experts, mentors, and fellow participants provides long-term connections with the global climate and sustainability community.
  • Funding and logistical support: Life of Pachamama covers airfare, accommodation, per diem, and travel insurance for the COP31 participation period, reducing financial barriers to global engagement.

Through these experiences, participants are equipped to lead climate initiatives in their territories, contribute to intergenerational dialogue on climate issues, and support local implementation of strategies aligned with national and global sustainability goals.

Eligibility

To apply, candidates must meet all of the following requirements:

  • Be between 15 and 30 years old from April 6, 2026, until November 20, 2026.
  • Have nationality or legal status from one of these countries: Colombia, Mexico, Peru, Brazil, Indonesia, Ghana, Gabon, Uganda, or Tanzania. Refugees or climate migrants from these countries may also apply with proper documentation.
  • Reside in one of the eligible countries or demonstrate verified refugee or climate migrant status.
  • Be part of an indigenous, peasant, Afro-descendant, or rural community, or be a victim of armed conflict.
  • Demonstrate interest in climate, gender, human rights, and diplomacy, and commitment to knowledge democratization in their territories.
  • Possess leadership or activism experience in socio-environmental issues, with or without higher education.
  • Have access to a computer and internet to participate in virtual training.
  • Be able to dedicate at least four hours per week to online training sessions between May and August 2026 (GMT-5).
  • Hold a passport valid until at least May 20, 2027, or commit in writing to obtaining one if selected.
  • Be proficient in Spanish or English.
  • While not mandatory, adherence to recommended vaccination schedules of the country of origin is strongly advised.

Candidates are also expected to have strong analytical, written, and oral communication skills, and a willingness to actively participate in all training sessions, collaborative activities, and post-COP community initiatives.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants are required to submit a video presentation and written materials according to the guidelines specified by Life of Pachamama. The selection process occurs in multiple phases:

  • Phase 1: Application deadline is April 30, 2026. Shortlisted candidates are notified on May 8, 2026, and training runs from May 23 to July 11, 2026.
  • Phase 2: Selected candidates complete mentorship from August to October 2026 and attend COP31 from November 9–20, 2026.
  • Phase 3: Participants implement local climate initiatives, with proposals due December 1–23, 2026, and implementation in 2027.

Selected individuals are expected to actively participate in training, deliver policy briefs, present position speeches, and contribute to the democratization of knowledge in their communities.

 To learn more and apply, visit the official Life of Pachamama website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Life of Pachamama Youth Climate Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation 2026–2027
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
IThe UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation is a fully financed, internationally respected fellowship program offering young archaeologists and conservation professionals from selected developing member countries the unique chance to advance both academic and practical expertise in heritage preservation and archaeological sciences. This opportunity combines multidisciplinary academic learning with applied practice at one of […]

The post UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation 2026–2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
IThe UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation is a fully financed, internationally respected fellowship program offering young archaeologists and conservation professionals from selected developing member countries the unique chance to advance both academic and practical expertise in heritage preservation and archaeological sciences. This opportunity combines multidisciplinary academic learning with applied practice at one of Poland’s leading archaeological faculties — Adam Mickiewicz University in Poznań.

Overview: What Is the UNESCO/Poland Archaeology and Conservation Fellowship?

Since its inception under UNESCO’s Fellowship Programme umbrella, the UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation has aimed to build human resource capacity in archaeological research and cultural heritage conservation. The program is implemented jointly by UNESCO, the Polish National Commission for UNESCO, and the Faculty of Archaeology at Adam Mickiewicz University (UAM) in Poznań, Poland.

This fellowship is designed to foster multicultural academic exchange, elevate professional standards, and encourage international cooperation in the crucial fields of archaeology and heritage preservation. Seven (7) fellowships are available for the 2026–2027 cohort and will run for nine months from 1 October 2026 to 30 June 2027.

Who Is This Fellowship For? Eligibility and Qualifications

This fellowship is specifically targeted at emerging professionals in archaeology and conservation from select countries. To qualify, applicants must meet rigorous academic and professional criteria.

Eligible Countries

The program is open only to nationals of the following UNESCO‑invited member states:

  • Africa & Middle East: Algeria, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Egypt, Ghana, Senegal, Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, Republic of South Africa
  • Americas: Bolivia, Cuba, Ecuador, Jamaica, Peru
  • Asia & Eurasia: Georgia, Iran (Islamic Republic of), Iraq, Kazakhstan, Mongolia, Uzbekistan
  • Europe & Others: Moldova

These invited states were chosen to promote capacity building in regions which are often underrepresented in global archaeological research, including post‑conflict nations where heritage conservation is especially critical.

Academic and Professional Criteria

To apply for this fellowship, candidates must:

  • Hold at least a Bachelor’s (B.A.) or Master’s (M.A.) degree — preferably in Archaeology, Heritage Conservation, or a closely related discipline.
  • Demonstrate proficiency in reading and writing English (spoken proficiency is advantageous).
  • Be no older than 40 years of age at the time of application.

These eligibility requirements ensure that applicants are prepared to engage with advanced course content and rigorous professional activities during the nine‑month training period.

Fellowship Experience: What You Will Study and Gain

Accepted fellows will undertake a nine‑month immersive archaeological internship at the Faculty of Archaeology, Adam Mickiewicz University in Poznań, Poland.

Training Structure

The program includes:

  • Core academic courses focusing on the latest multidisciplinary methods in archaeological practice.
  • Supplementary trainings and workshops tailored to heritage conservation and site methodology.
  • Research activities and potential participation in local fieldwork under the supervision of experienced faculty.

Through this blend of classroom and field learning, fellows not only strengthen their technical skillsets but also increase their ability to work across international heritage contexts.

Step‑by‑Step Guide: How to Apply

Applying to this fellowship requires careful preparation and coordination with your National Commission for UNESCO. Because this is an intergovernmental programme, individual applications directly to UNESCO will not be accepted.

Step 1: Review Official Programme Documentation

  • Download and thoroughly read the Terms and Conditions, Application Form, and Study Programme Annexes. These documents outline the precise requirements, rules, and expectations for applicants.

Step 2: Contact Your National UNESCO Commission

Your country’s National Commission for UNESCO will be responsible for:

  • Issuing nomination and endorsement of your application.
  • Ensuring that your submission meets all specified criteria.

Locate contact details for your National Commission via UNESCO’s official directory.

Step 3: Prepare Your Application Package

You will need the following in English:

  1. Completed UNESCO fellowship application form (all four pages).
  2. Recent passport‑style photo.
  3. Scanned copy of passport ID page.
  4. Certified copies of academic degrees (B.A or M.A).
  5. UNESCO certificate of language knowledge (if English is not your native language).
  6. Two letters of recommendation validating your competence and suitability.

Submit your complete application to your National Commission before it forwards candidates to UNESCO.

Step 4: National Commission Nomination to UNESCO

After your National Commission endorses your application, it must be submitted to UNESCO by 15 May 2026. Only endorsements received by this date will be considered in the selection process.

Apply here

What Winners Receive: Comprehensive Fellowship Benefits

This fellowship provides a robust combination of academic, logistical, and financial support across several key areas.

Support from Adam Mickiewicz University (Poland)

  • Tuition coverage for the full nine‑month period.
  • Access to university facilities, academic resources, and libraries.
  • Monthly allowance:
    • 2,200 PLN (Polish Zloty) for M.A. holders.
    • 1,800 PLN for B.A. holders.
  • One‑time special allowance upon arrival (matching the monthly rate), intended to support cultural visits, conferences, workshops, or settling‑in expenses.

Recipients should plan to manage living costs in Poland within these allowances.

Support from UNESCO

  • International travel costs for the fellowship journey to and from Poland.
  • Health insurance coverage for the duration of the fellowship for medically fit fellows.
  • A one‑time pocket allowance of US$120

Visa and Practical Considerations

  • Fellows from countries with a Polish Embassy or Consulate must obtain a visa before departure.
  • Applicants from countries without such diplomatic representation should secure a visa through the nearest Polish Embassy or Consulate.
  • Visa and passport expenses are not covered by UNESCO or the Government of Poland.

Why This Fellowship Matters: Professional, Academic & Cultural Impact

The UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation is far more than a training program — it is a career‑transforming platform that:

  • Elevates the technical expertise and international credibility of young professionals.
  • Promotes cross‑cultural exchange and understanding among emerging heritage leaders from diverse regions.
  • Strengthens global networks and future collaborations in archaeological research and conservation practice.

If you meet the eligibility criteria and have a strong commitment to archaeology or heritage conservation, this fellowship could be a defining milestone in your professional journey.

Deadline

15th May 2026

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post UNESCO/Poland Co‑Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation 2026–2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Jerusalem Fund Summer 2026 Internship Program in Washington, DC
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Jerusalem Fund for Education and Community Development is offering its Summer 2026 Internship Program, an intensive 11-week, in-person internship based in Washington, DC. Running from June 1 to August 14, 2026, this program is designed for students and recent graduates who are deeply interested in Palestine, the Middle East, and the role of the […]

The post Jerusalem Fund Summer 2026 Internship Program in Washington, DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Jerusalem Fund for Education and Community Development is offering its Summer 2026 Internship Program, an intensive 11-week, in-person internship based in Washington, DC. Running from June 1 to August 14, 2026, this program is designed for students and recent graduates who are deeply interested in Palestine, the Middle East, and the role of the United States in the region.

The Jerusalem Fund is a nonprofit organization dedicated to increasing awareness about Palestine and supporting Palestinian communities both locally and globally. Its work is structured across three core programs: The Palestine Center, which focuses on educational outreach and policy analysis; The Humanitarian Link, which addresses humanitarian and development efforts; and Gallery Al-Quds, which promotes cultural preservation and storytelling.

Interns in this program are not treated as assistants but as junior colleagues. Participants are placed into one primary portfolio area based on their interests and skills. These include War, Conflict, and Politics; Tech Policy; Arts and Culture; and Humanitarian Aid and Community Resilience. Each portfolio offers a unique lens through which interns can engage with real-world issues while contributing meaningful work.

Throughout the internship, participants will engage in research, writing, public programming, outreach, and archiving projects. The program also includes cohort sessions, mentorship, and exposure to Washington’s policy and nonprofit environment.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This internship is particularly significant for young professionals seeking to engage critically with global issues, especially those related to conflict, culture, and international policy. It provides a rare opportunity to work within an organization that integrates research, humanitarian work, and cultural programming under one mission.

By contributing to real projects that address complex political and social issues, interns gain a deeper understanding of how narratives are shaped and how policy and public discourse are influenced. The program also emphasizes ethical storytelling and responsible research, which are critical skills in today’s information landscape.

Globally, opportunities like this help bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world application. They empower young people to become informed voices in international conversations and to contribute meaningfully to social and political discourse.

What You’ll Gain

This internship provides a comprehensive professional development experience for those interested in policy, research, and international affairs. Interns receive direct mentorship and feedback from experienced professionals, allowing them to refine their analytical and writing skills in a real-world context.

Participants gain hands-on experience in producing research outputs such as essays, reports, and public-facing content. They also develop a strong understanding of nonprofit operations, policy analysis, and cultural programming. Exposure to Washington, DC’s policy and media ecosystem provides valuable insight into how international and domestic issues intersect.

Interns will also benefit from academic and career coaching, helping them plan future academic or professional pathways. By the end of the program, participants will have built a portfolio of work that can support applications for graduate school, fellowships, or careers in policy, media, or international development.

Additional benefits include the opportunity to present at the end-of-summer Interns’ Conference, eligibility for the Hisham Sharabi Prize for Best Writing, and a stipend of $1,200 upon successful completion of the program.

Eligibility

The program is open to current undergraduate students and recent graduates who demonstrate a strong interest in Palestine, the Middle East, and related global issues.

Applicants should have strong writing, research, and analytical skills, along with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. A demonstrated interest in public-facing work, intellectual curiosity, and professionalism are essential.

While not mandatory, applicants with backgrounds in fields such as political science, international relations, journalism, media studies, sociology, or related disciplines are encouraged to apply. Additional skills such as familiarity with digital tools, design platforms, or emerging technologies like AI may strengthen an application.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Jerusalem Fund Summer 2026 Internship Program, applicants must prepare and submit a complete application package.

Required materials include a cover letter, resume, transcript, writing sample, ranked portfolio preferences, and the names or letters of two professional references.

Applicants must complete the application form available on the official platform and upload all required documents as instructed. If there are any issues with the application form, candidates may submit their materials via email. It is recommended to carefully review the official website for the correct submission process and contact details.

The application deadline is May 1, 2026. Late applications are typically not accepted, so candidates are encouraged to prepare and submit their materials well in advance.

👉 To learn more and apply, visit the Jerusalem Fund Internship job post.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Jerusalem Fund Summer 2026 Internship Program in Washington, DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paragon Policy Fellowship Summer 2026: Remote Opportunity in Tech Policy and Government Innovation
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Paragon Policy Fellowship Summer 2026 is a part-time, fully remote fellowship designed for students and recent graduates interested in technology policy and public service. Organized by Paragon, the program connects fellows with state and local government partners across the United States to work on real-world policy challenges at the intersection of technology, governance, and […]

The post Paragon Policy Fellowship Summer 2026: Remote Opportunity in Tech Policy and Government Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Paragon Policy Fellowship Summer 2026 is a part-time, fully remote fellowship designed for students and recent graduates interested in technology policy and public service. Organized by Paragon, the program connects fellows with state and local government partners across the United States to work on real-world policy challenges at the intersection of technology, governance, and public interest.

This fellowship focuses on addressing pressing issues such as artificial intelligence governance, broadband access, data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital public services. Participants collaborate directly with government partners to research, analyze, and develop actionable policy solutions that can be implemented in real-world settings.

The Summer 2026 cohort will run from May 27 to August 14, 2026. Fellows are expected to commit approximately 8–10 hours per week, balancing independent work with team meetings and collaboration sessions. While the fellowship is remote and flexible, it requires consistent engagement and participation in scheduled activities.

It is important to note that due to funding transitions, the Summer 2026 fellowship is an unpaid, volunteer opportunity.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As governments increasingly rely on technology to deliver services and regulate innovation, there is a growing need for professionals who understand both policy and technology. The Paragon Policy Fellowship addresses this need by preparing early-career individuals to contribute meaningfully to public interest technology.

The program provides a practical pathway for students and graduates to enter the field of tech policy, which can often be difficult to access without prior experience. By working directly with government partners, fellows gain insight into real decision-making processes and contribute to solutions that can improve public services and community outcomes.

This experience is particularly valuable for those interested in careers in public policy, civic technology, digital governance, and social impact. It also supports the development of more inclusive and effective technology policies by bringing diverse perspectives into the policymaking process.

What You’ll Gain

Participants in the Paragon Policy Fellowship gain practical experience working on real policy challenges that impact communities. Fellows develop a strong understanding of how governments approach emerging technology issues and how policy decisions are shaped and implemented.

The program offers hands-on experience in policy research, writing, and analysis, along with exposure to real government workflows. Fellows also benefit from mentorship through workshops, speaker sessions, and guidance from professionals working in technology policy and public service.

In addition to technical and analytical skills, participants build valuable professional networks. Fellows connect with peers, alumni, and leaders in the tech policy space, creating long-term opportunities for collaboration and career growth.

The fellowship also provides access to the broader Paragon network, including future opportunities in research, media, and professional development.

Eligibility

Applicants must meet all of the following criteria:

  • Candidates must be currently enrolled in a degree program (undergraduate, graduate, J.D., or Ph.D.) or have graduated within the past year.
  • Applicants must primarily reside in the United States and must have valid work authorization. Accepted categories include U.S. citizenship, permanent residency, or specific visa types such as F-1 OPT, CPT, STEM OPT, or J-1 visas. International students must ensure their visa allows off-campus work authorization.
  • Applicants must be able to commit 8–10 hours per week throughout the fellowship duration and participate in regular virtual meetings with their teams and government partners.
  • Candidates must not have previously completed the Paragon Policy Fellowship.
  • Participants must also be available for the full program duration and for the group trial interview stage, which takes place between May 17 and May 20, 2026.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Paragon Policy Fellowship Summer 2026, candidates must complete the online application form available on the official Paragon platform.

Applicants are required to provide personal and academic information, respond to several short essay questions, and submit a recent expository writing sample (3–5 pages). The application also includes questions about availability, experience, and areas of interest in technology policy.

After submitting the written application, selected candidates will be invited to participate in a group trial interview, scheduled between May 17 and May 20, 2026.

All applicants must ensure that they meet the eligibility requirements and can provide proof of work authorization if selected.

The application deadline is April 30, 2026, at 8:59 PM Pacific Time / 11:59 PM Eastern Time. Late applications are not accepted, so early submission is strongly recommended.

To learn more and apply, visit the official Paragon Policy Fellowship application page.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Paragon Policy Fellowship Summer 2026: Remote Opportunity in Tech Policy and Government Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
